title: Beach/Venice daytime demand likely above normal
parking_lot: 100 Venice Way; 121 Windward; 29 Windward; 601 Ocean Front Walk; 801 Ocean Front Walk; 801 Speedway; 909 Ocean Front; 909 Garage; 46 Market; 5 Dudley; Pardee; 25 Rose; 220 Rose; 1501 Main; 407 Colorado
notes: Sunny weather should lift beach and tourist demand above a normal Tuesday.
reason: Sunny weather row has positive impact, and coastal businesses peak from late morning through afternoon.
suggested_action: Watch Venice/Ocean Front/Boardwalk lots from late morning through afternoon. Keep normal pricing unless entries start running high.

title: SoFi World Cup event may raise Westside/Hollywood pressure
parking_lot: 11801 W Olympic Blvd Parking; 139 South Beverly Parking; 6125 Hollywood Blvd Parking
notes: FIFA World Cup Round of 32 at SoFi Stadium is in the weekly CSV.
reason: The SoFi event is not next door to the coastal lots, but it can create regional traffic pressure and raise demand around West LA, Beverly Hills, and Hollywood.
suggested_action: Watch these lots earlier than the event time because arrivals and traffic can start hours before.

title: Dodgers vs Padres may add evening regional traffic
parking_lot: 6125 Hollywood Blvd Parking; 139 South Beverly Parking; 11801 W Olympic Blvd Parking
notes: Dodgers vs Padres game is in the weekly CSV.
reason: The game is not directly next to these lots, but it can add evening traffic and regional demand pressure.
suggested_action: Treat this as a traffic pressure note for Hollywood, West LA, and Beverly. Do not over-apply it to Venice beach lots.

title: Holiday weekend buildup likely starts before July 4
parking_lot: Beach, Venice, Santa Monica, Rose, Main Street, and Washington/Marina lots
notes: Friday before July 4 with sunny/warm weather and holiday weekend demand rows.
reason: Beach, restaurant, and nightlife demand should start building on Friday before the actual holiday.
suggested_action: Start watching coastal lots before lunch. Increase attention on dinner/nightlife lots after 5 PM.

title: Hollywood nightlife likely elevated Friday night
notes: Hollywood nightlife weekend surge is in the weekly CSV.
reason: Holiday weekend nightlife can continue after midnight.
suggested_action: Watch evening and late-night utilization. Keep this separate from daytime tourist demand.

title: Critical July 4 beach demand surge
parking_lot: 100 Venice Way; 121 Windward; 29 Windward; 601 Ocean Front Walk; 801 Ocean Front Walk; 801 Speedway; 909 Ocean Front; 909 Garage; 46 Market; 5 Dudley; Pardee; 25 Rose; 220 Rose; 1501 Main; 407 Colorado; Gladstones
notes: July 4 holiday, hot/sunny weather, and beach visitor surge rows all stack together.
reason: This is the biggest watch day for Venice, Santa Monica, Rose, Main Street, Ocean Front, and beach-adjacent lots.
suggested_action: Watch starting in the morning. Expect demand to stay elevated through evening. Consider higher pricing and extra staffing for beach-adjacent lots.

title: Dodgers game overlaps with July 4 regional demand
parking_lot: 6125 Hollywood Blvd Parking; 139 South Beverly Parking; 11801 W Olympic Blvd Parking
notes: Dodgers vs Padres and July 4 holiday effects overlap.
reason: Holiday behavior plus sports traffic can make these lots behave differently from a normal Saturday.
suggested_action: Watch Hollywood and Westside lots in the afternoon. Do not assume normal Saturday behavior.

title: Post-holiday Sunday beach demand likely remains high
parking_lot: Beach, Venice, Santa Monica, Rose, Main Street, and Washington/Marina lots
notes: Sunday beach/boardwalk visitor surge and sunny weather remain in the CSV.
reason: Demand should stay elevated after July 4, especially late morning through early evening.
suggested_action: Expect a slower start than July 4 but strong midday/afternoon demand.

title: Dodgers Sunday game may keep Hollywood/Westside pressure elevated
parking_lot: 6125 Hollywood Blvd Parking; 139 South Beverly Parking; 11801 W Olympic Blvd Parking
notes: Dodgers vs Padres Sunday game remains in the CSV.
reason: Regional traffic pressure may continue, but coastal demand is likely the bigger driver for beach lots.
suggested_action: Monitor, but prioritize coastal lots unless real entries show Hollywood/Westside spikes.
